quizQuestion: Which Namco designer created the 1980 arcade classic Pac-Man?
explanation: Toru Iwatani designed Pac-Man, released by Namco in 1980. He reportedly was inspired by a pizza with a missing slice. The game went on to generate $12 billion in cumulative revenue. Source: Wikipedia/Pac-Man

quizQuestion: Approximately how much cumulative revenue has Pac-Man generated since its 1980 release?
explanation: Pac-Man has generated approximately $12 billion in cumulative revenue across arcade machines, home ports, merchandise, and spin-offs. The original arcade game sold about 43 million units. Source: Wikipedia/Pac-Man

quizQuestion: Which Soviet software engineer created Tetris in 1984?
explanation: Alexey Pajitnov created Tetris in 1984 while working at the Soviet Academy of Sciences. He initially received no royalties; the game became a commercial sensation in 1988 and has sold over 520 million copies across all versions. Source: Wikipedia/Tetris

quizQuestion: How many copies of Tetris have been sold across all versions, making it the second-best-selling game series?
explanation: Tetris has sold over 520 million copies across all platforms and versions, making it the second-best-selling video game series ever. It was first released commercially in 1988 and remains widely played today. Source: Wikipedia/Tetris

quizQuestion: Which Nintendo designer created Super Mario Bros. in 1985?
explanation: Shigeru Miyamoto designed Super Mario Bros., released for the NES in 1985. The game sold over 58 million copies and a 1990 study found Mario was more recognizable to American children than Mickey Mouse. Source: Wikipedia/Super_Mario_Bros.

quizQuestion: A 1990 study found that Mario was more recognizable to American children than which iconic Disney character?
explanation: A 1990 survey found that Mario was more recognizable among American children than Mickey Mouse, illustrating Nintendo's cultural dominance during that era. Super Mario Bros. had sold over 58 million copies by that point. Source: Wikipedia/Super_Mario_Bros.

quizQuestion: The Legend of Zelda (1986) was the first NES cartridge to feature what technology that let players save their progress?
explanation: The Legend of Zelda (1986) was the first NES cartridge to use battery-backed saving, allowing players to save their progress between sessions. Previously, players had to use passwords or start over. It sold 6.51 million copies. Source: Wikipedia/The_Legend_of_Zelda_(video_game)

quizQuestion: Sonic the Hedgehog helped which Sega console outsell the Super Nintendo 2:1 in 1991-1992?
explanation: Sonic the Hedgehog (1991) was so popular it helped the Sega Genesis outsell the Super Nintendo 2:1 during 1991-1992, a remarkable feat in the console wars era. Sonic sold over 40 million copies across the series. Source: Wikipedia/Sonic_the_Hedgehog_(video_game)

quizQuestion: In the 1981 arcade game Donkey Kong, Mario made his debut under what original name?
explanation: Mario first appeared in the 1981 Donkey Kong arcade game under the name 'Jumpman.' The game was designed by Shigeru Miyamoto and generated $180 million by June 1982. Jumpman was later renamed Mario in later games. Source: Wikipedia/Donkey_Kong_(video_game)

quizQuestion: The original PlayStation became the first console to exceed how many units sold?
explanation: The original PlayStation, released in Japan in 1994, sold 102.49 million units — making it the first console to exceed 100 million units sold. It was designed under Ken Kutaragi, who became known as 'the father of PlayStation.' Source: Wikipedia/PlayStation_(console)

quizQuestion: Which Sony engineer, often called 'the father of PlayStation,' led the design of the original PlayStation?
explanation: Ken Kutaragi was the primary architect of the PlayStation gaming platform and is widely known as 'the father of PlayStation.' The original PlayStation (1994) sold 102.49 million units and was the first console to surpass 100 million units sold. Source: Wikipedia/PlayStation_(console)

quizQuestion: Which Sony console, with 160 million units sold, remains the best-selling video game console of all time?
explanation: The PlayStation 2, released in 2000, sold over 160 million units and remains the best-selling video game console in history. It launched over 4,000 titles during its lifespan. Source: Wikipedia/PlayStation_2

quizQuestion: With 155 million units shipped, which 2017 Nintendo console is the second-best-selling video game console ever?
explanation: The Nintendo Switch, released in 2017, has shipped over 155 million units, making it the second-best-selling video game console of all time behind only the PlayStation 2. Its hybrid home/portable design was a major innovation. Source: Wikipedia/Nintendo_Switch

quizQuestion: Which bundled Nintendo game with motion controls sold 82.90 million copies, making it the third-best-selling game ever?
explanation: Wii Sports (2006), bundled with the Nintendo Wii, sold 82.90 million copies and is the third-best-selling video game of all time. Its motion controls revolutionized casual gaming and helped the Wii reach a broader audience than any prior Nintendo console. Source: Wikipedia/Wii_Sports
distractors: The Legend of Zelda: Twilight Princess

quizQuestion: Which 1989 Nintendo handheld, bundled with Tetris, sold 118.69 million units including the Color variant?
explanation: The Game Boy (1989) sold 118.69 million units combined with the Game Boy Color. Its bundling with Tetris was a key sales driver, and it dominated the handheld market for a decade. Source: Wikipedia/Game_Boy

quizQuestion: The Nintendo 64 controller (1996) was the first game controller to include a standard what, enabling true 3D movement in games?
explanation: The Nintendo 64 (1996) controller introduced the first standard analog stick on a mainstream console controller, enabling fluid 3D movement that became foundational for modern gaming. Time magazine named it 'Machine of the Year.' Source: Wikipedia/Nintendo_64

quizQuestion: At its 2010 peak, how many subscribers did World of Warcraft have?
explanation: World of Warcraft peaked at 12 million subscribers in 2010, making it the dominant massively multiplayer online game. It has generated over $9.23 billion in revenue since launching in 2004. Source: Wikipedia/World_of_Warcraft

quizQuestion: Which studio, known for real-time strategy games, released World of Warcraft in 2004?
explanation: Blizzard Entertainment released World of Warcraft in 2004. Having previously made StarCraft and Warcraft III, Blizzard made the leap to MMORPGs with massive success, attracting 12 million subscribers at its 2010 peak. Source: Wikipedia/World_of_Warcraft

quizQuestion: Who created the sandbox game Minecraft, released through Mojang in 2011?
explanation: Markus 'Notch' Persson created Minecraft, officially released in 2011 through his studio Mojang. It has sold over 350 million copies, making it the best-selling video game in history. Microsoft acquired Mojang for $2.5 billion in 2014. Source: Wikipedia/Minecraft

quizQuestion: How many copies has Minecraft sold, making it the best-selling video game in history?
explanation: Minecraft has sold over 350 million copies across all platforms, making it the best-selling individual video game ever. Creator Markus 'Notch' Persson sold Mojang to Microsoft in 2014 for $2.5 billion. Source: Wikipedia/Minecraft

quizQuestion: How many concurrent viewers watched the 2019 League of Legends World Championship, demonstrating esports' mainstream scale?
explanation: The 2019 League of Legends World Championship drew over 100 million concurrent viewers, rivaling traditional sports broadcasts. Riot Games' League of Legends generated $150 million per month in revenue in 2016. Source: Wikipedia/League_of_Legends

quizQuestion: How many players did Fortnite attract in its first year after launching in 2017?
explanation: Fortnite attracted over 125 million players in its first year after launching in 2017, and generated over $9 billion in revenue through 2019. A Ninja and Drake livestream became the most-watched gaming stream at the time. Source: Wikipedia/Fortnite

quizQuestion: Which American studio, also known for the Unreal Engine, developed Fortnite?
explanation: Fortnite was developed by Epic Games, which is also famous for creating the Unreal Engine — used by countless other developers. Fortnite's battle royale mode launched in 2017 and rapidly became a cultural phenomenon. Source: Wikipedia/Fortnite

quizQuestion: Which 2018 social deduction game by Innersloth became a COVID-19 pandemic phenomenon with 500 million players by November 2020?
explanation: Among Us was released in 2018 but went largely unnoticed until the COVID-19 pandemic drove 500 million players to the game by November 2020. The game's social deduction mechanics — finding impostors among crewmates — made it perfect for online play. Source: Wikipedia/Among_Us

quizQuestion: How many downloads did Pokémon GO achieve in its first 33 days after launching in 2016?
explanation: Pokémon GO reached 100 million downloads in just 33 days after its July 2016 launch, setting a record at the time. The augmented reality game pioneered mass-market AR gaming and generated over $6 billion in revenue. Source: Wikipedia/Pokémon_Go

quizQuestion: Which augmented reality company, spun out of Google, developed Pokémon GO?
explanation: Niantic, a company spun out of Google in 2015, developed Pokémon GO in partnership with The Pokémon Company. The game pioneered mass-market AR gaming and generated over $6 billion in revenue. Source: Wikipedia/Pokémon_Go

quizQuestion: From how many publications did Elden Ring (2022) win Game of the Year awards?
explanation: Elden Ring, released in 2022 by FromSoftware and directed by Hidetaka Miyazaki, won Game of the Year from over 300 publications. George R.R. Martin contributed to the game's worldbuilding. It sold 30 million copies. Source: Wikipedia/Elden_Ring

quizQuestion: Which author of A Song of Ice and Fire contributed worldbuilding lore to Elden Ring?
explanation: George R.R. Martin, best known for Game of Thrones, collaborated with FromSoftware director Hidetaka Miyazaki on Elden Ring's world lore. The game won over 300 Game of the Year awards and sold 30 million copies. Source: Wikipedia/Elden_Ring

quizQuestion: Which Belgian studio developed Baldur's Gate 3, the first game to win Game of the Year at all 5 major gaming ceremonies?
explanation: Larian Studios, based in Belgium, developed Baldur's Gate 3 (2023). It became the first game ever to win Game of the Year at all five major gaming ceremonies, including The Game Awards, BAFTA, GDC, DICE, and IGF. It sold over 20 million copies. Source: Wikipedia/Baldur%27s_Gate_3

quizQuestion: Grand Theft Auto V earned $1 billion in how many days after launch — setting a record as the fastest entertainment product to do so?
explanation: Grand Theft Auto V (2013) earned $1 billion in just 3 days, making it the fastest entertainment product ever to reach that milestone — surpassing even major film releases. It has since sold 225 million copies. Source: Wikipedia/Grand_Theft_Auto_V

quizQuestion: How many copies has Grand Theft Auto V sold since its 2013 release?
explanation: Grand Theft Auto V has sold approximately 225 million copies since its 2013 release, making it one of the best-selling video games in history. It generated $1 billion in its first 3 days alone. Source: Wikipedia/Grand_Theft_Auto_V

quizQuestion: Which Sony-owned studio developed The Last of Us (2013), directed by Neil Druckmann?
explanation: Naughty Dog, a Sony-owned studio known for the Uncharted series, developed The Last of Us (2013) under director Neil Druckmann. The game earned a 95 Metacritic score and was later adapted into a hit HBO series. Source: Wikipedia/The_Last_of_Us

quizQuestion: Which developer created Guitar Hero in 2005, sparking the rhythm game boom?
explanation: Harmonix developed the original Guitar Hero (2005), which sparked the rhythm game boom of the mid-2000s and grew into a $1 billion franchise. Harmonix later developed Rock Band. Source: Wikipedia/Guitar_Hero

quizQuestion: Which Finnish studio, facing bankruptcy before its hit game, developed Angry Birds in 2009?
explanation: Rovio, a Finnish mobile game developer, created Angry Birds in 2009 after a string of failures nearly drove the company into bankruptcy. The game reached 50 million downloads and spawned films, theme parks, and merchandise. Source: Wikipedia/Angry_Birds

quizQuestion: By what percentage did North American video game revenue decline during the crash of 1983, falling from $3.2 billion to roughly $100 million?
explanation: The video game crash of 1983 was catastrophic: North American revenue collapsed from $3.2 billion to just $100 million — a 97% decline. The Nintendo Entertainment System, released in 1985, rescued the market and restored consumer trust. Source: Wikipedia/Video_game_crash_of_1983

quizQuestion: Which console, released in North America in 1985, rescued the video game industry after the catastrophic 1983 market crash?
explanation: After the 1983 video game crash wiped out 97% of North American revenue, the Nintendo Entertainment System (released in North America in 1985) rescued the industry. Nintendo's strict quality licensing system prevented the market flooding that caused the crash. Source: Wikipedia/Video_game_crash_of_1983
